resources.string: Shows the status of background tasks
Charts will appear here once the simulation is complete.

Price comparisons will appear here. 

 Select usage(s) and costs from the other tabs to control the content of this tab

You can sort the table, and select the fields to show.

Tilting the screen (landscape) will better fit more fields.

Progress of recently added costs and usages can be seen in the notification area.

Selecting a comparison will pop up a usage graph (pie chart) that shows how much electricity was purchased at each rate

"The usage profile captures how electricity is used. Use the six buttons to configure the usage profile.

Usage profiles must include at least a load profile for the house (yellow highlight when missing). Where you specify hourly, daily and monthly distribution as well as the annual and base loads. There are several sources for distribution.

This tab is also where you can describe your (planned) solar installation.Panel(s) and inverter(s) are needed for electricity generation. 

Batteries, hot water systems and electric vehicles can also be configured, scheduled and, for water and EV's, diverted.

Once configured and saved, a simulation will run. Simulation results will appear in the details tab shortly after. Simulation progress is visible in the notification area. "

There are no price plans created.

At least one usage price plan is needed for the application to simulate and compare. 

Price plans are a standard way to describe the contract with your supplier. You can add as many suppliers and plans as you like. 

You can add a price plans manually using the '+' button in the bottom right, and using the menu, download or load from a file. 

Once created, you can delete, copy and view/edit usage profiles from this tab. 

Selecting a usage will add it to the comparison tab.

There are no usage profiles created.

At least one usage profile is needed for the application to simulate and compare. 

Usage profiles allow you to describe how you use electricity. You can add as many usage profiles as you like.

You can add a usage profile manually using the '+' button in the bottom right, and using the menu, download or load from a file. 

Once created, you can delete, copy and view/edit usage profiles from this tab. 

Selecting a usage will add it to the comparison tab.
Excess solar diversion to water heating set
Cannot find solar data for these panels. Remember to fetch or update solar data for each set of panels.
Distribution of electricity usage by day of week
Distribution of electricity usage by hour of day
Distribution of electricity usage by month of year
Add a new water heating schedule

You may download costs and usages by supplying a URL. The default URLs are aimed to get you started. There is no guarantee that contents are correct. It is advisable to check the costs and usages for suitability and correctness.
Checking for data for this location and panel string.
The data in the DB for this panel string was updated. You may change the location or return to the panels. A bar chart will appear there shortly.
PVGIS data was downloaded, now adding to the DB.
PVGIS is being queried for data that matches the location.
The location has changed. A DB update is needed to confirm the change.
There is data in the DB for this panel string. You may change the location or return to the panels. A bar chart will appear there shortly.
Show or hide the menu bar and tabs
Please select something in the filter
Data update in progress, try again in a moment
Individual devices — assign a role so the simulator can model the device (Ignore = leave in the flat load)
Hide the parts of the app you don\'t use. Hiding never deletes anything — a hidden component a scenario already has still simulates, and hidden sources keep their data.
Applies to the wizard and the dashboard accordions.
This is the %1$s edition, but your device looks like %2$s. Tariffs and currency are region-specific — if that\'s not right, install the edition for your country instead.

"Solar data is variable. This app uses historical solar data in estimations.

Solar panels may be shaded. This app makes no attempt to consider shading.

Price plan accuracy determines calculation accuracy. Check price plan details.

Price plans do not include Public Services Obligations. Estimates will not include this.

Price plans do not include rate usage limitations or complex contracts. Estimates will be wrong where limitations are exceeded or conditions triggered.

All estimates are based on user input. If the input is bad, the output will be too.

This app provides the ability to explore possibilities, estimations are not advice (financial or otherwise). The app is provided as-is, no representation or warranty of any kind, express or implied, regarding the accuracy, adequacy, validity, reliability, availability, or completeness of any information is made.

Enjoy!"
Inline help text on each tab
Sample loaded · simulation running in background
Community-maintained — may be out of date. You can edit anything after importing.
Couldn\'t download the list — check your connection and retry.
No list for your supplier? Have an AI assistant build one:

1. Copy the prompt below.
2. Paste it into a text editor (Notes, etc.) — it's too long to edit inside most LLM chat boxes.
3. Fill in the supplier and country at the top.
4. Copy the edited prompt and paste it into your favourite LLM (ChatGPT, Claude, Gemini…).
5. Copy the JSON it returns, then switch to "Paste JSON" here.
I have the JSON — paste it
Heads-up: this only works some of the time — LLM results are hit-and-miss and often need fixing. Always check the generated rates against a recent bill, and edit any plan after importing.
Pick a file or paste JSON to preview.

plurals.name: ui2_scenarios_delete_all_body
plurals.item: The scenario will be permanently deleted, along with its simulation and costing results. Data sources are not affected. There is no undo.
All %1$d scenarios will be permanently deleted, along with their simulation and costing results. Data sources are not affected. There is no undo.

resources.string: See whether solar and a battery would pay off for you, fast. Answer a couple of questions and tap Calculate.
✓ Imported — yearly total read from your data.
Download an HDF file from the ESBN Networks portal (My Energy Consumption → Download). We read your exact yearly total and half-hourly usage pattern from it — the most accurate option.

It\'s on your bill as kWh used per year. A typical Irish home is around 4,200 kWh. We spread it over the year using a standard smart-meter pattern. For a more accurate result, switch to “My ESBN data”.

Adjust in 0.5 kWp steps — a typical home install is around 7 kWp. We model it at 40° tilt and fetch a year of expected output for your exact spot from PVGIS (an EU solar dataset). Turn solar off to see the battery-only case.
0 = north, 90 = east, 180 = south, 270 = west. South is best in Ireland.
We need your location to estimate solar yield (PVGIS).
A home battery stores daytime solar (or cheap night-rate electricity) to use at peak times. Try a few sizes to see which pays off — bigger isn\'t always better.
Fills the battery on a cheap night-rate window so you draw less at peak times. Best paired with a night-saver tariff.
Tap Calculate to run the simulation and see your cost.
Something went wrong. Check your inputs and try again.
Add real supplier tariffs to see your cost.
Costs can only be shown against real tariffs. Download the community-maintained list (it may be out of date — you can edit the plans afterwards).

plurals.name: ui2_simple_tariffs_loaded
plurals.item: %1$d supplier tariff loaded · community-maintained, may be out of date.
%1$d supplier tariffs loaded · community-maintained, may be out of date.

resources.string: Internally the app stores every reading in UTC. The zone you pick here decides how imports without explicit zone info are interpreted, and how UTC values are rendered back to you. Default is your phone\'s current zone — change it only if you want to keep seeing home time while travelling.
If a plan with the same name already exists, should the imported version replace it, or be kept alongside the existing one?
If a scenario with the same name already exists, should the imported version replace it, or be kept alongside the existing one?
Every tariff stored in the app, including day-rate schedules.
Every saved scenario with all of its components — load profile, PV, batteries, hot water, EV.
A full SQLite snapshot — all plans, scenarios, data sources, and precomputed simulation results. Re-imports byte-for-byte on another device.
Pick which scenarios and data sources to include. All supplier plans are bundled so imported scenarios can find their tariffs.
Pick a JSON file (or paste) to add plans to your library. Plans whose name matches an existing one can be replaced or kept alongside.
Pick a JSON file (or paste) to add scenarios to your library. Scenarios whose name matches an existing one can be replaced or kept alongside.
Pick a .db file exported from another device. The file is validated against the current schema before any rows are written; you\'ll see a preview and a Replace toggle before commit.
Accepts the JSON shape produced by the Share button on a plan, or the bulk export above.
Accepts the JSON shape produced by the Share button on a scenario, or the bulk export above.
Could not read the picked file
Could not validate the picked file
No scenarios or data sources available to export.
Costings, panel data, sim time series. Recomputable from inputs.
PV data (paneldata, fetched from PVGIS) and load profile time-series are imported with each scenario. Costings and simulation outputs are regenerated locally from the imported inputs.
Restart the app after importing for data sources to appear.
Replace existing data with the same name / serial
Off: keep what you have, skip imports that collide.

Move plans and scenarios between devices, share them with someone, or back them up to your file storage. For exporting a single plan or scenario use the Share button on its row; for importing one into a wizard step, the Import option on each accordion is more focused than the bulk paths here.
Note: when a database snapshot includes data sources, restart the app after importing for those sources to appear.
Comparison results are exported from the Compare tab itself — pick your sources, scenarios and plans, then use the Share button on each result panel.
· restart the app to see data sources
Components shared by 2 or more scenarios. Editing one updates every linked scenario; changes are cached until you Save.
Give this scenario its own editable copy of the component.
This component type cannot be unlinked from the Directors — fork it instead.
Which %1$s item would you like to share?
Pick a scenario whose %1$s to share. It becomes the seed — link more scenarios to it afterwards.
No scenario has an unshared %1$s to share.
You have pending link / unlink / fork edits. Closing now will discard them.
No shared %1$s components. Tap + to start sharing one.
Push another source\'s stored history into Home Assistant\'s hourly statistics — fix gaps or bad meter data. Preview compares what HA holds against what will be written before anything changes.
Connect Home Assistant first (Data Source Management → Home Assistant).
No other source has stored data to push.
Overwrites the selected range of your HA sensor statistics (hourly); later totals are shifted so history stays consistent.
Overwrites the selected range of your HA sensor statistics (hourly). Discover sensors first.
Writes comparetout:* statistics alongside your sensors — non-destructive, easy to remove in HA.
Hours where Home Assistant disagrees with the source are marked ⚠. Pick one to compare the values and backfill just that hour.
Tap a row to see its details · Star marks your current contract

Every cost in the app — Compare tab totals, dashboard Tariff Plan tables, KPIs — is calculated against one of these plans. Tap the ★ on a plan to mark it as the tariff you\'re currently contracted to; it will be highlighted everywhere it appears.\n\nThe green ✓ marks a plan as active. Active only narrows the dashboard\'s Tariff Plan table — the Compare tab still evaluates every plan regardless of its active state. Open a plan and tap the ✓ to toggle: grey → green activates it, green → grey deactivates it.
\"%1$s\" will be permanently deleted. Any cached costing results for this plan will also be removed.

plurals.name: ui2_ppl_delete_all_body
plurals.item: All %1$d plan will be permanently deleted, along with any cached costing results. There is no undo.
All %1$d plans will be permanently deleted, along with any cached costing results. There is no undo.

resources.string: Active — tap to deactivate for the dashboard
Active — included in dashboard tariff tables
Inactive — tap to activate for the dashboard
Usage restrictions — rate changes past a cap
★ This is your current plan — highlighted in every Tariff Plan view.
Tap ★ to mark this as your current plan.
Tap “+ Create new” to enter your supplier\'s tariff. Plans drive every cost calculation in the app — at least one is needed before the Compare tab can show prices.
Accepts the JSON shape produced by the Share button on a plan, or a bulk export.

Fetches every Octopus Energy tariff open for sign-up in your region and adds each as a supplier plan. Dynamic tariffs (Agile/Tracker) are skipped; the export rate assumes Outgoing Fixed — edit any plan after importing.
Fetching tariffs — this walks every open product…
Couldn\'t resolve a region — check the postcode or pick a region below.
Couldn\'t resolve a region — pick one below.
Duplicate a source or simulation to compare it against itself over different timeframes. Set the Timeframe to “Per subject” to give each copy its own range.
Selection opens in full-screen pickers to keep this view tidy.
Greyed series aren\'t recorded by any selected source — full inverter feeds (e.g. AlphaESS) and simulations provide them, meter-only sources (e.g. ESBN HDF) don\'t.
Advanced adds PV/battery flow breakdowns plus bonus & fixed cost.
Switch to Advanced for PV/battery flows and more cost columns.
Advanced: a trailing window ending on the chosen day.
Basic: whole calendar day / month / year.
One range applies to every subject
Each source / simulation has its own range
Pick D / M / Y / * to set this range
All time — every reading on file
Tap a column header to sort. Tap again to reverse.
Pie is always small-multiples — one pie per subject.
Axis granularity picked from each subject\'s timeframe — short ranges get Day, long ranges get Month or Year.
Each subject\'s chart uses the chosen %1$s buckets.
Bar axis: bucketed when split, or merged with ≤ 2 series; otherwise one bar per subject (totals).
Bar in %1$s buckets when split, or merged with ≤ 2 series; otherwise totals.
Each subject gets its own chart.
All subjects share one chart for direct comparison.
Tap to choose sources, sims & plans
Tariffs to price the energy flows against
Pick at least one cost column in Filter.
Pick at least one energy flow in Filter.
Each row is a source or simulation priced against a plan. Columns show %1$s in %2$s — tap a header to sort. Rows: %3$s.
Grouped bars in %1$s. Each group along the bottom is one subject; the coloured bars within it are %2$s. Subjects: %3$s.

Each bar shows one plan\'s cost in %1$s: buy split into its rate bands plus any fixed charge stack up from the zero line; sell hangs below it. The marker is the net — above the line is a cost, below is a credit. Subjects: %2$s.
Each bar stacks %1$s in %2$s for one subject — a taller bar means more in total. Subjects: %3$s.
Each line follows %1$s month by month (%2$s), one line per subject — see the Sources legend for line colours. Subjects: %3$s.
Filled area showing %1$s month by month (%2$s), one chart per subject. Subjects: %3$s.
Each pie splits one subject\'s %1$s into %2$s; the subject is named beneath its pie. Subjects: %3$s.
No data for the chosen subjects and timeframe.
Pick at least one source or simulation, a filter and a timeframe to see results.
Pick at least one source/simulation and a plan, a filter and a timeframe to see results.
None — plan prices apply without usage caps
Any unsaved changes to this supplier plan will be lost.
Accepts the JSON shape produced by the Share button on a plan, or a single plan from a bulk export. The wizard will be pre-filled — you can edit anything before saving.
This file contains %1$d plans. Pick the one to load into the wizard. (To import them all into the library instead, use the drawer\'s Import / Export screen.)
The current details, charges and day-rates will be replaced with the contents of the JSON. The plan will still save back to the same row when you tap Save.
Pre-fill the wizard from a shared plan or bulk-export file.
Couldn\'t save. Fix the highlighted issues and try again.
Plans tagged with another country than the phone are hidden and deactivated.
Supplier estimates your export instead of measuring it.

A restriction caps how many kWh the plan sells at a rate within a period; usage beyond the cap is charged the revised price. Restrictions attach to a rate value — if you change that band\'s price, re-attach the restriction.
Costings honour the usage caps below.

Each day rate covers a date window and a set of weekdays. Add costs (%1$s) and assign time ranges to each — together they must cover the full 24-hour day. Across all day rates, the dates must tile the calendar year and every weekday must be covered.
Date ranges overlap. Trim one of the start/end dates so each calendar day belongs to exactly one day-rate.
Missing date coverage: %1$s. Add or extend a day rate to cover these days.
These weekdays aren\'t covered by any day rate sharing this date window: %1$s
Add a load profile in the Usage Data step so the simulation has consumption to model.
Solar yield is fetched per panel from its location. Open PV System to generate it, or wait if a simulation is already running.
Real weather is fetched from Copernicus CDS when the simulation runs. Open Heat Pump to check the weather source / CDS token, or wait if a fetch is already running.
Costs and charts appear once the simulation finishes calculating. It runs automatically after you edit a simulation or its tariffs.
After this update, solar generation for these imported source(s) couldn\'t be refreshed automatically and needs re-importing: %1$s.
Runs once after the update — you can keep using the app; figures fill in when it finishes. This message disappears when the update is complete.
Tap for the times this happened

Grid import is limited by your supply\'s MIC (%1$s kW). The model never clamps it, but flags any interval that exceeds it so you can spot breaker/fuse/penalty risk and decide whether to upgrade your connection or shift load.

plurals.name: ui2_dash_mic_body
plurals.item: Your simulation drew more than your Maximum Import Capacity (MIC) of %1$s kW in %2$d five-minute interval. The model still meets the load — the grid physically can — but in reality exceeding your MIC can trip the main breaker, blow a fuse, or incur supplier penalties. Open the graphs for these days to see what drove the peak; to fix it, raise your MIC or shift flexible loads (EV / hot water) away from these times.

Your simulation drew more than your Maximum Import Capacity (MIC) of %1$s kW in %2$d five-minute intervals. The model still meets the load — the grid physically can — but in reality exceeding your MIC can trip the main breaker, blow a fuse, or incur supplier penalties. Open the graphs for these days to see what drove the peak; to fix it, raise your MIC or shift flexible loads (EV / hot water) away from these times.

resources.string: No solar data for this period
Results aren\'t ready yet — the breakdown appears here once the simulation finishes calculating. See \"Needs attention\" above for what\'s outstanding.
Solar data is still being generated, so these figures may be incomplete.
Solar data hasn\'t been generated for these panels yet. It\'s fetched automatically when the simulation runs.
%1$s kWh, stop at %2$s%%, charge %3$s kW / discharge %4$s kW
Tap a row to update the band chart →
"Tap a row to see tariff band breakdown ↗"
No band breakdown for this plan
No active price plans — activate one via Supplier Plans
Nothing to draw yet — configure components in the wizard.
No data for the selected filter.
The scenario or data source this dashboard was set to is no longer available. Open the navigation drawer and pick a scenario or data source to view.
Pick how you\'d like to start:
EU JRC solar irradiance · no account needed
ERA5 reanalysis for heat-pump weather · API key required

Each location/orientation is fetched once from PVGIS and cached here, then re-scaled to every panel that shares it. Add a PV array to a scenario and run it, and the cached download appears in this list.
Set your Copernicus CDS Personal Access Token, then the heat-pump section can fetch real weather for your location. Each fetched (location, period) is cached here.

Real-world energy data flows from your inverter or smart meter into the dashboard and Compare tab. Set credentials, pick which system to track, and decide whether the app should refresh automatically each day. Stats and graphs themselves live in the dashboard.
AlphaESS uses your AlphaCloud OpenAPI keys — generate them in the developer portal.
Set up the Energy dashboard in Home Assistant first — that\'s where these sensors come from.
Push another source\'s history into HA statistics
%1$d grid in · %2$d grid out · %3$d solar · %4$d battery
Tap Re-discover after enabling the Energy dashboard in HA.
EV / hot water / heat pump devices feed scenario derivation; \"Remove from load\" also subtracts the device\'s energy at import (any role).
Set credentials first to discover sensors.
ESBN cloud sync is deprecated. Download your HDF file from the ESBN customer portal and import it below.
Imported MPRNs will appear here once a file has been ingested.

Octopus needs your account number (A-XXXXXXXX) and an API key from the Octopus dashboard (Account → API access). Meter points are discovered automatically; fetching also builds supplier plans from your region\'s tariffs.
Meter points (MPANs) will appear here once credentials are validated.

Solis needs an API Key ID and secret from soliscloud.com (Account → API Management — raise a service ticket if the menu is missing). Stations are discovered automatically; data updates every 5 minutes on the Solis side.
Stations will appear here once credentials are validated.
This deletes the cached data for every %1$s in this source. PV is re-fetched the next time you save a scenario that uses it. There is no undo.
This permanently deletes all stored readings for this source AND its saved credentials. You\'ll need to re-enter them to use it again. There is no undo.
This permanently deletes all stored readings and configuration for this source. There is no undo.
Choose how much data to remove. Credentials and system configuration are preserved either way.
No data has been recorded for this system yet.
This permanently removes every reading for \"%1$s\". There is no undo.
Tap a start date, then an end date
Every reading between %1$s and %2$s for \"%3$s\" will be permanently removed. There is no undo.
Pick a date · catch-up runs up to %1$s
Crosses midnight — runs %1$s until %2$s next day.
How much power the charger draws from the grid during the window.
EV charges before other solar divert loads (e.g. hot water).
Cabling, inverter, soiling, mismatch. 14% is PVGIS\'s default.
Data for these settings is already in the system · will be visible after save
Will be fetched from PVGIS in background after save
On save: processed in the background with a progress notification.
Rated kWp of the source. Data is scaled when it differs from the string total (%1$s kWp).
Rotate the source generation curve to match this string\'s azimuth.
Azimuth of the source string (0/360=N · 90=E · 180=S · 270=W). Target: %1$d°.
\"%1$s\" is not in the inverter list — pick one above.
Add an Inverter above to link this battery.
Charge curve — % of max rate at each SOC band
\"%1$s\" is not in the battery inverter list — pick one above.
Applies to every battery attached to this inverter.
Charge stops once the battery reaches this state of charge.
How fast the immersion can put energy into the tank.
Temperature drop per day with no top-up — depends on tank insulation.
Mark the hours when hot water gets used and what share of the day\'s total each draw takes. Defaults model a typical morning shower / midday rinse / evening peak.
Tap a chart to zoom in
You have unsaved changes. Leave anyway?
Tap any section to expand it. Start and Usage Data are required to run a simulation.
How do you want to begin?
How much electricity does the home use?
EV charge schedules and solar divert
Model a heat pump from your current heating
This will clear the Usage Data and EV sections. Your simulation name will be kept. Continue?
Accepts the JSON shape produced by the Share button on a scenario, or a single scenario from a bulk export. The accordions will be pre-filled — you can edit anything before saving.
This file contains %1$d scenarios. Pick the one to load into the wizard. (To import them all into the library instead, use the drawer\'s Import / Export screen.)
Every simulation starts from one of four places. Pick what fits.
Start with empty sections and build up.
Duplicate a saved simulation, then edit freely.
Re-use components from a saved simulation.
Paste or pick a scenario JSON file to pre-fill every section.
A unique name — shown everywhere in the app.
One of 6 pre-built Irish residential profiles.
Import distribution from another simulation, then edit freely.
Set hourly, daily and monthly weightings manually.
Re-use a profile from another simulation. Changes in source propagate here.
Choose how the load profile is built. Annual usage (kWh) is always required.
Derived automatically from how the profile was created.
Tap to set hourly, daily and monthly usage patterns.
Total electricity used in a year.
Highest single-hour draw from the grid.
Highest single-hour export — set by your contract.
No imported data found. Import smart-meter or inverter data first.
Uses the actual measured load for the chosen year, mapped onto the 2001 simulation grid.
Builds an average hourly/daily/monthly shape over the chosen window.
ESBN provides import/export only (no solar or load readings). Load is estimated from grid import — this will be inaccurate if solar panels or a battery are present.
Patch missing days using a standard load profile.
Each battery stores excess solar (or off-peak grid) energy for use later. Add one battery per physical unit; multiple batteries on the same inverter are fine.
Optional — skip if you have no battery storage.
Charge from the grid during cheap windows (e.g. night-saver) so the stored energy covers expensive peak hours. Add a window for each cheap-rate period; leave empty if you only want solar charging.
Add a battery above to enable charge scheduling.
Add one if your tariff has cheap off-peak hours worth pre-charging from.
"⚠ Schedules below target an inverter with no batteries — add a battery to make them effective."

Force the battery to export to the grid during a chosen window — useful when feed-in rates are higher than the avoided import cost (peak-export tariffs). Leave empty for standard self-consumption only.
Add a battery above to enable discharge scheduling.
Add one if your tariff has high feed-in rates worth exporting to.
Configure the hot-water cylinder so the simulation can model when the immersion is needed and how much excess solar can be diverted to it.
Optional — skip if hot water isn\'t part of this scenario.
Windows when the immersion is allowed to run from the grid (e.g. cheap night-rate). Leave empty if you only want hot water from solar divert.
Add a tank above to enable scheduling.
Add one if your tariff has a cheap window worth heating the tank in.
Send excess solar to the immersion instead of exporting it. Free hot water when the panels are producing more than the house is using.
Divert excess solar to hot water
Add a tank above to enable solar divert.
Add scheduled EV charging windows. Each window defines when the car charges and at what rate.
This section is optional — skip it if you don\'t have an EV.
Divert surplus solar energy to charge the EV within a defined time window.
Optional — add if you want to charge the EV from excess solar.
Estimate the electricity a heat pump would use in place of your oil or gas boiler.
How much heat the home needs — from your current fuel use, or estimated from the building fabric for a new build.
The heat pump unit\'s rated efficiency and heat output.
Where the outdoor weather for the hourly simulation comes from, plus how the home responds to wind.
Whole-house heat loss, fabric + ventilation. A compliant new build is ~0.7–1.2.
Ballpark — the final demand comes from the hourly simulation.
Leave equal to today\'s setpoint to keep current comfort.
% of the fuel that was space heating, not hot water (blank = use the default).
Seasonal efficiency of the boiler you\'re replacing.
Today\'s setpoint — used to rescale demand if the desired temperature differs.
Outdoor temperature below which the home needs heating.
Seasonal efficiency averaged across the year.
Max heat output; the backup heater covers any shortfall.
Outdoor temperature the rated COP is quoted at.
How much COP changes per °C outdoors.
A draughty home loses heat fastest on cold, windy days — that can push a heat pump past its capacity onto the expensive backup heater.
The 2001, Ireland sample lets you try the heat pump now; CDS fetches real weather for your location.
Uses the 2001 reference year. Add real PV data from a historical source (in the PV System section) to align weather to your measured year.
The period matches your imported PV data so weather and solar share the same year.
Location defaults to your PV array — edit for a different site.
Fetching real weather needs a Copernicus CDS Personal Access Token. Add it under Data Source Management, then choose CDS here.
Add solar inverters. Each inverter can serve multiple panel arrays and batteries.
This section is optional — skip it if you have no solar or battery.
Add panel strings. Each string is an independent array with its own orientation and data source.
This section is optional — skip it if you have no solar panels.

plurals.name: ui2_wiz_pvgis_banner
plurals.item: PVGIS data for %1$d panel string is being downloaded in the background. The dashboard PV System card will show solar data once complete.
PVGIS data for %1$d panel strings is being downloaded in the background. The dashboard PV System card will show solar data once complete.

resources.string: Required: Start and Usage Data. EV is optional.
Accepts a load-profile JSON or a whole scenario JSON. Annual usage and all distributions will be replaced.
Replaces the current inverter list. Accepts an inverter JSON array or a whole scenario JSON.
Replaces the current panel-string list. Accepts a panel JSON array or a whole scenario JSON.
Replaces batteries, charge and discharge schedules together — schedules reference inverter and battery names. Accepts a battery slice or a whole scenario JSON.
Replaces hot-water system, schedules and divert. Accepts a hot-water slice or a whole scenario JSON.
Replaces EV charge schedules and diverts. Accepts an EV slice or a whole scenario JSON.
Replaces the heat pump. Accepts a heat-pump JSON array or a whole scenario JSON.
No supplier plans found in the JSON.
No scenarios found in the JSON.
Parsed %1$d supplier plans — pick one to load
Couldn\'t find a load profile in the JSON.
No inverters found in the JSON.
No PV panel strings found in the JSON.
No heat pump found in the JSON.
No battery setup found in the JSON. Expected a scenario JSON or an object with \"Batteries\"/\"LoadShift\"/\"DischargeToGrid\".
No hot-water setup found in the JSON. Expected a scenario JSON or an object with \"HWSystem\"/\"HWSchedule\"/\"HWDivert\".
No EV setup found in the JSON. Expected a scenario JSON or an object with \"EVCharge\"/\"EVDivert\"/\"EVDiverts\".
Parsed %1$d scenarios — you\'ll pick one to load
Location is needed to estimate solar yield
Couldn\'t get a location fix — try again outdoors
Calculate first to create a scenario
End date must come after start date
Scenario not saved — the name is already in use.
